seoTitle: Create Reusable Power BI Templates (PBIT Files)
metaDescription: Learn how to create Power BI templates (PBIT files) so you can reuse report layouts and standardize reporting across teams and projects.
intro: Rebuilding the same report structure for every new project wastes time that a good template can save entirely. This guide covers building your first Power BI template, saving it as a PBIT file, and reusing it across future projects. It's aimed at anyone responsible for keeping report formatting consistent across a team or department.

steps.heading: Create your first template
steps.body: Build out a report with the layout, visuals, and measures you want to standardize, using placeholder or sample data as the base structure.

steps.heading: Save as a PBIT file
steps.body: Use Power BI's Save As option and choose the Power BI Template format, which strips out the underlying data but keeps the structure and formatting.

steps.heading: Understand major use cases
steps.body: Templates are especially useful for recurring report types like monthly financial summaries or standardized sales dashboards used across regions or teams.

steps.heading: Review standardization benefits
steps.body: A shared template ensures every team producing a similar report follows the same visual style and calculation logic, reducing inconsistency across departments.

faq.question: What's the difference between a PBIX and a PBIT file?
faq.answer: A PBIX file contains your report along with the actual data, while a PBIT template file contains only the structure, visuals, and queries without the data itself.

faq.question: Does opening a PBIT file require reconnecting to data sources?
faq.answer: Yes, when you open a PBIT template, Power BI will prompt you to enter data source parameters or credentials so it can load fresh data into the structure.

faq.question: Can I share a PBIT template with people who don't have my data access?
faq.answer: Yes, since the template doesn't include the actual data, it's safe to share broadly, though recipients will need their own access to the data sources referenced in the queries.

faq.question: How do I update a template once it's already in use by a team?
faq.answer: You'll need to update the original PBIX report, re-export it as a new PBIT file, and redistribute it, since existing templates don't update automatically.
